description: Vegetation placement test vectors — verifies tree placement grid, density thresholds, altitude exclusion zones, stream exclusion, tree types, scale ranges, collision radius, and tussock alpine-only rule.

vectors.description: Tree placement grid spacing: potential tree positions are on a 3.0m grid within each chunk, with jitter applied.

expected.potentialPositionsPerChunk: approximately 11 x 11 = 121 grid cells per chunk (floor(32/3)+1 per axis)
expected.note: Trees are placed on a regular grid with deterministic noise-based jitter. Not all grid positions produce a tree — density noise must exceed threshold.

vectors.description: Tree density threshold: a tree is placed only if treeNoise2D(x * 0.05, z * 0.05) > 0.15.

expected.placementProbability: roughly 42.5% of grid positions (assuming uniform noise distribution above 0.15 in [-1,1])
expected.formula: density = treeNoise2D(worldX * 0.05, worldZ * 0.05); skip if density <= 0.15
expected.note: The density value also drives tree type and scale selection.

vectors.description: No trees below SHORE_LEVEL (-2.8m): terrain at or below shore level is sandy/underwater and has no vegetation.

expected.note: Shore zone (-3.5 to -2.8) and underwater zone (< -3.5) have no trees. This prevents trees from floating in water or appearing on sandy beaches.

vectors.description: No trees above TREELINE_START + 2 (18m): trees are excluded above this altitude. Tussock (type 3) is placed above TREELINE_START but uses the same exclusion ceiling.

expected.note: Between 16-18m, trees exist but are progressively smaller (down to 30% scale). Above 18m, no tree instances at all.

vectors.description: Stream exclusion: no trees where streamFactor > 0.15. The terrain shader computes streamFactor from the valley channel noise (domain-warped ridge noise).

expected.exclusionRule: Trees should not be placed where stream channels are active
expected.streamFactorSource: smoothstep over vStreamChannel attribute from getRiverFactor()
expected.terrainShaderThreshold: smoothstep(0.3, 0.5, vStreamChannel) for water core rendering
expected.note: Stream exclusion prevents trees from spawning in valley channels. The exact threshold depends on the placement code's use of getStreamFactor(). River carving currently disabled but valley channels still affect terrain.

vectors.description: Tree types: 4 types indexed 0-3. Type is determined by density value. Above subalpine, type is forced to 0 (pine). Above treeline, type becomes 3 (tussock).

types.name: pine
types.description: 5 stacked open-ended cones, dark cool green

types.name: oak
types.description: 6 clustered icosahedron spheres, warm rich green

types.name: birch
types.description: 4 open-ended cone lobes, white bark, bright yellow-green

types.name: tussock
types.description: 56 PlaneGeometry blade strips, golden-straw, alpine only

expected.note: Types 0-2 are full trees. Type 3 (tussock) reuses tree instancing but is alpine ground cover.

vectors.description: Scale range: tree base scale is 2.5 to 6.0, computed from density value.

expected.note: Higher density noise produces larger trees. At treeline, trees shrink to 30% of computed scale.

vectors.description: Tree collision radius: trunks have a collision radius of 0.4m for player collision detection.

expected.note: Player cannot walk through tree trunks (types 0-2). Collision disabled while swimming. Tussock (type 3) has no collision — player walks through.

vectors.description: Tussock (type 3) only above TREELINE_START (16m). Tussock is NZ red tussock — golden-straw alpine ground cover.

properties.geometry: PlaneGeometry blade strips merged into dome

expected.note: Tussock reuses tree instancing infrastructure. The vestigial trunk is invisible at tussock's small scale. Above 18m (tree exclusion ceiling), no tussock either.

vectors.description: Tree planting depth: trees are planted 0.15m below terrain height so trunks emerge naturally.

expected.note: Prevents visible gap between trunk base and ground. The 0.15m burial ensures the trunk appears to grow from the soil.

vectors.description: Altitude scale transition: tree scale reduces linearly from full at 10m to 30% at 18m (TREELINE_START + 2).
